menu_options.node: 🔌 Physical node (a Sensmos device on your network)
menu_options.data: 🟣 Data only (push HA sensors to the map — no hardware)

node.title: Add Sensmos node
node.description: Enter your node's address and PIN. The address is shown in the Sensmos app (e.g. 192.168.68.71 or sensmos-ab12cd.local).

data.title: Data only — push to the map
data.description: Selected Home Assistant sensors will show up on the live map as a software node (purple). No hardware, no wallet. After adding, pick which sensors to publish in the integration's options.

data.key: Passkey (min 32 chars) — this is your node's identity, keep it safe
data.label: Map label (optional)

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the node. Check the address and that the node is online.
error.key_too_short: Passkey must be at least 32 characters.

menu_options.feed_pub: ➕ Feed a native entity (pub.*) from HA
menu_options.feed_own: ➕ Feed a custom entity (own.*) from HA

feed_pub.title: Pick a native node entity
feed_pub.description: These are entities the network rewards. Pick one — in the next step you'll choose a matching Home Assistant sensor.

feed_pub_entity.title: Pick the Home Assistant sensor
feed_pub_entity.description: Feeding **{node_entity}** (unit: {unit}). The list is filtered to compatible sensors; units are converted automatically (e.g. kW → W).

feed_own.title: Feed a custom entity
feed_own.description: Any HA sensor or binary sensor can be stored on the node as an own.* entity for use in node scripts. The unit is taken from the HA entity automatically; binary sensors (e.g. motion) become 1/0.

data.name: Name on the node (e.g. boiler → own.boiler)
data.ha_entity: Home Assistant sensor or binary sensor

subscribe.title: Subscribe to another node
subscribe.description: Your node will receive the other node's data (billed daily in GALU). You'll see what's available before confirming.

subscribe_confirm.title: Confirm subscription

data_add.title: Publish a Home Assistant sensor
data_add.description: Pick an HA sensor and the Sensmos entity name. Use **pub.<native>** for a name from the native list (categorized + heatmap), or **own.<anything>** for custom data. Examples: pub.batt_soc, pub.pm25, own.boiler_temp.

data.ha_entity: Home Assistant sensor or binary sensor

data_settings.title: Data settings
data_settings.description: Location is optional — without it the server uses GeoIP from your IP.

error.invalid_name: Invalid name — use letters, digits, underscore.
error.target_unavailable: Could not fetch the target node's catalog.
error.invalid_entity: Use pub.<name> or own.<name> (lowercase, e.g. pub.batt_soc).

abort.no_native_left: All native entities are already mapped.
abort.subscribe_ok: ✅ Subscribed! New sensors will appear shortly.
abort.insufficient_balance: Not enough GALU for the first day of the subscription.
